OkaySpace Game Engine — Changelog

v2.127.0  (Turn-based / RPG components, Steam Workshop + Cloud, OkayEngine rename)
  - RPG / turn-based building blocks: three new components under an "RPG / Turn-Based"
    Add Component category, each with a full inspector and scene serialization.
      * Stats     — health/mana, level + XP (auto level-up that raises caps and refills),
                    strength/defense/intelligence/agility; TakeDamage mitigates by defense
                    (min 1), fires on_death; AddXP fires on_level_up.
      * Inventory — capacity-limited slots that auto-stack; Add/Remove/Count/Has, fires
                    on_inventory_changed.
      * TurnManager — ordered participants with turn/round tracking; Start/EndTurn fire
                    on_turn_start, on_round_start; SetTurn/Remove for combat flow.
  - Steam Workshop (UGC): publish/update community items, subscribe/unsubscribe, browse
    with WorkshopQuery, and resolve installed item folders. Works in the in-memory
    simulation for development; the real backend maps to ISteamUGC. New steam_workshop_*
    OkayScript builtins.
  - Steam Cloud: added file enumeration (CloudFiles) and CloudEnabled, plus
    SteamManager pass-throughs and steam_cloud_has / steam_cloud_delete / steam_cloud_count
    script builtins. The real backend maps Cloud to ISteamRemoteStorage.
  - Renamed the editor executable to OkayEngine.exe (was OkaySpaceEngine.exe). The launcher
    still finds older installs by name, and the window title is now "OkayEngine".

v2.126.0  (Launcher updater fix)
  - The launcher updater never downgrades: it only fetches a build when the published
    version is strictly newer than what's installed, and the startup auto-check shows the
    local version instead of silently pulling an older binary. Fixes "it downloads 2.97".

v2.125.0  (Third-person shooter scene template)
  - New "Third Person Shooter" starter scene (Templates::ThirdPersonShooter): a
    playable over-the-shoulder level with a directional light, a ground, cover crates,
    a row of shootable target spheres, and the player wired with the
    ThirdPersonShooterController + camera. Available in New Project / templates next to
    First Person and Third Person.

v2.124.0  (Build Settings: graphics / quality + cursor lock)
  - The Build Game dialog gained a Graphics / Quality group so a shipped game ships
    with the look you want: per-pixel lighting, shadows, bloom, ambient occlusion,
    FXAA, and 2x anti-aliasing — plus a Lock cursor option (hide + lock on launch).
  - These are written into game.okayconfig and applied by the player at startup
    (renderer effect toggles + supersample factor + Cursor lock), giving full control
    over a built game's quality without touching code.

v2.123.0  (Third-person SHOOTER controller + cursor lock/hide)
  - New ThirdPersonShooterController: over-the-shoulder aim camera, the body always
    faces where you look (strafe shooter), mouse look, right-mouse aim (camera zooms
    in over the shoulder), left-mouse fire (calls the script's on_fire()). Momentum +
    forgiving jump + camera collision, like the other controllers.
  - New Unity-style Cursor API (Cursor.lockState / .visible / Capture(on)). The player
    applies it: locked = hidden, centred cursor with relative-motion mouse look; free =
    normal cursor. The shooter locks the cursor on play (toggle: Lock Cursor).
  - GameObject > Player > Third Person Shooter premade, Add Component entry, full
    inspector, scene serialization. New shooter test; 78 tests pass.

v2.122.0  (fix controller jump, FPS default no 2x AA, uncapped vsync)
  - First/third-person jump works again in every scene (grounded uses the reliable
    vertical-velocity check, not contacts-only); fixes stuck-in-fall-animation.
  - 3D anti-aliasing 2x OFF by default (it rendered 4x the pixels — the ~10 FPS cause,
    bad on HiDPI). Cheap FXAA still smooths edges; opt back in under View.
  - VSync off by default = uncapped FPS (no 60 Hz cap); toggle under View > VSync.

v2.121.0  (packaging: consistent names, one entry point, no stale auto-download)
  - The build now produces the exact exe names the tools expect: the launcher is
    OkaySpace.exe (the one thing to double-click), the editor OkaySpaceEngine.exe, the
    runtime OkaySpacePlayer.exe. This stops the launcher from thinking the engine was
    "missing" and silently downloading an OLDER copy under a different name (the source
    of duplicate exes and the stuck-on-2.97 version).
  - The launcher no longer phones home on startup: the published GitHub build can lag
    this one, so an auto-check used to install an older engine and mislead with "up to
    date". Updates are now opt-in via the button; the footer shows the bundled version.
  - Organized install layout: OkaySpace.exe + SDL2.dll at the top, the editor + player
    tucked in an Engine/ subfolder. The launcher searches that subfolder, and the
    editor finds the launcher one level up for its gate.

v2.120.0  (packaging: Unity-style — ship SDL2.dll)
  - The Windows build now links SDL2 dynamically and ships SDL2.dll beside the
    executables (Unity-style), instead of static-linking it into every exe. The C/C++
    runtime (libgcc/libstdc++/winpthread) stays static, so SDL2.dll is the ONLY DLL
    needed — no MinGW-runtime DLL pile.
  - Result: much smaller binaries (editor 19 MB -> 8 MB, player 19 -> 6, launcher
    16 -> 4) that share one 1.8 MB SDL2.dll. CMake copies SDL2.dll into the output bin
    dir automatically. Configure with -DOKAY_STATIC_SDL=ON for the old single-exe build.

v2.119.0  (UI: soft / blurred drop shadows)
  - UIPanel and UIButton gain a Shadow Blur (shadowSoftness): 0 = a crisp shadow,
    higher = a soft penumbra for the premium card look. The player fakes the blur by
    stacking a few expanding, fading copies of the shape behind the widget.
  - Inspector blur sliders + back-compatible serialization. uishape suite extended
    (softness round-trip); 78 tests pass.

v2.118.0  (UI: segmented tab control)
  - New UITabs component: a segmented control / tab bar — a row of equal segments
    where exactly one is selected (settings tabs, difficulty pickers, view switches).
    Clicking a segment sets `value` and calls the script's on_change(). Pill/rounded
    silhouette, track + selected + per-state text colors, edit the tab list inline.
  - Hit-testing maps a click to the right segment (shared SegmentAt() used by the
    player and tests). Add Component entry, inspector, and scene serialization.
    uishape suite extended (segment mapping + select clamp + round-trip); 78 tests pass.

v2.117.0  (no-clip: transform-only players collide with walls/floors)
  - Players moved by the Transform (no Rigidbody3D) no longer clip through geometry.
    New Physics3D::ResolveSphere depenetrates a body sphere out of solid colliders
    (box/sphere/capsule, ignoring triggers and the player itself), and a shared
    ResolvePlayerBody helper applies it after movement.
  - Wired into all four 3D controllers (First/Third Person, Top Down, Click To Move)
    on their transform-only path, so every player kind is stopped by walls and floors
    even without a physics body. (Rigidbody players still collide via physics.)
  - New test: a transform-only player is stopped by a wall instead of passing through.
    78 tests pass.

v2.116.0  (new Top-Down controller + premade player)
  - New TopDownController (twin-stick / ARPG / RTS-hero): WASD/left-stick movement on
    the XZ plane with momentum (accel/decel), turn-to-face-travel, world- or
    camera-relative axes, and a fixed high, angled follow camera (distance / pitch /
    yaw / damping). Drives a Character's walk/run animation.
  - GameObject > Player > Top Down drops a fully wired top-down player into the scene
    (Templates::AddTopDownPlayer), plus an Add Component entry and full inspector.
  - Back-compatible serialization. New top-down move + premade tests; 78 tests pass.

v2.115.0  (controllers polished + premade-player shortcuts)
  - First-person controller now matches third-person feel: movement momentum
    (accel/decel + air control) and a forgiving jump (grounded-from-contacts +
    coyote time + jump buffer) replacing the old apex double-jump check.
  - Click-to-move controller eases to a stop over an Arrive Radius so arrivals no
    longer jolt.
  - New "GameObject > Player" shortcuts: Third Person / First Person / Click To Move
    drop a fully wired player (Character + controller + Rigidbody3D + collider, and a
    camera — child-mounted for first person) straight into the current scene. Backed
    by reusable Templates::Add*Player helpers.
  - All new fields are in the inspector and saved with the scene (back-compatible).
    New premade-player test; 78 tests pass.

v2.114.0  (third-person controller: feel + no camera clipping)
  - Movement now has momentum: horizontal velocity eases toward the target with
    tunable acceleration/deceleration and reduced air control, so starts and stops
    have weight instead of snapping.
  - Reliable grounded detection from collision contacts, with coyote time (jump just
    after a ledge) and a jump buffer (press just before landing) — fixing the old
    mid-air double-jump at the jump apex.
  - Camera spring arm: the third-person camera casts to the player and pulls in when a
    wall/floor is in the way, so the view never clips through geometry (new
    Physics3D::Raycast ignore-parameter to skip the player). Smoother default follow.
  - All new fields are in the inspector and saved with the scene (back-compatible).
    New camera spring-arm test; 78 tests pass.

v2.113.0  (launcher: polish + editor launch gate)
  - Polished the launcher UI: modern rounded theme, generous spacing, the version is
    shown next to the logo and in the window title, card-style Play list and
    Marketplace entries, and a Tips panel on Create.
  - The editor is now gated behind the launcher: it only starts when opened via the
    launcher (which passes a --launcher <pid> handshake) AND that launcher is still
    running. Double-clicking the editor directly bounces the user to the launcher and
    exits. A development bypass (OKAY_DEV=1 or --dev) keeps local/CI builds runnable.

v2.112.0  (UI: radial spinner / indeterminate loader)
  - UIRadialProgress gains a Spin mode: the arc rotates continuously (spinSpeed deg/s)
    for an indeterminate loading spinner — with a small value it's the classic loader.
    EffectiveStart() feeds the renderer the live rotation; a Update() advances it.
  - Inspector Spin + Spin Speed controls, back-compatible serialization. uishape suite
    extended (spinner advance + round-trip); 78 tests pass.

v2.111.0  (UI: new Radial Progress widget — rings & pies)
  - New UIRadialProgress component: a circular/ring progress indicator (cooldowns,
    loaders, skill timers, score dials) — the radial counterpart to UIProgressBar.
    Draws a ring of configurable thickness (or a full pie) filled clockwise/counter
    from a start angle by value (0..1), with a track + fill color and optional percent.
  - The fill geometry is a single Sample() classifier (outside / track / fill) shared
    by the player renderer and unit tests, so the visible arc is well-defined.
  - GameObject > Add Component > UI Radial Progress, inspector controls, and scene
    serialization. uishape suite extended (arc classification + round-trip); 78 tests pass.

v2.110.0  (UI: rounded form controls — input fields + dropdowns)
  - UIInputField gains a shape + corner radius and an optional focus ring (a colored
    border that appears while the field is focused), for clean rounded text fields.
  - UIDropdown gains a shape + corner radius so the header renders as a rounded select.
  - The player draws both via the shape fill. Inspector controls + back-compatible
    serialization. uishape suite extended (input/dropdown round-trips); 78 tests pass.

v2.109.0  (UI: animated toggles — gliding switch + fading check)
  - UIToggle now animates: the Switch knob glides between off/on and the track
    cross-fades (animSpeed, eased and frame-rate independent), and the Checkbox tick
    fades in. AnimT() exposes the smoothed 0..1 state to the renderer.
  - The player renders the switch as a proper Pill track with a Circle knob and a
    rounded checkbox via the shape fill (no more hard rectangles).
  - Inspector Anim Speed control + back-compatible serialization. uishape suite
    extended (knob easing + round-trip); 78 tests pass.

v2.108.0  (UI: progress bars + sliders get shapes / gradients / round knobs)
  - UIProgressBar gains the shape system (Rounded/Pill capsule bars) and an optional
    gradient fill (e.g. green->yellow->red health) along the fill direction.
  - UISlider gains a track shape (pill/rounded) and a round-knob option for a clean
    circular handle.
  - The player renders both via the shape fill (track + fill + knob), so corner radius
    and pill ends actually draw. Inspector controls + back-compatible serialization.
    uishape suite extended (progress/slider round-trips); 78 tests pass.

v2.107.0  (UI: buttons get shapes + shadows + accurate hit-test)
  - UIButton gains the shape system (Rectangle/Rounded/Circle/Pill) and an optional
    drop shadow, for clean flat-UI / icon buttons. The player renders the silhouette
    (with border and shadow) instead of a plain box.
  - Button click hit-testing now follows the actual silhouette, so the corners of a
    rounded/circle/pill button aren't clickable where there's no pixel.
  - Inspector Shape selector + shadow controls, back-compatible serialization.
    uishape suite extended (circle-button hit-test + button round-trip); 78 tests pass.

v2.106.0  (UI: real shapes — rounded / circle / pill)
  - New UI shape system (UIShape): widgets can be a hard Rectangle, soft Rounded box
    (the modern flat look), a full Circle (avatars/badges) or a Pill/capsule (toggle
    tracks, tags). A single per-row span function drives both the fill and hit-testing.
  - The player now actually RENDERS rounded/circle/pill silhouettes (previously corner
    radius was stored but drawn as a plain box) via a scanline shape fill that also
    honors the drop shadow and border per shape.
  - UIPanel gains a Shape selector and a horizontal-gradient option; UIImage's
    textureless fallback gains a Shape so it can be a dot/badge/chip.
  - Inspector controls + back-compatible serialization. New uishape test suite
    (rectangle/circle/rounded/pill geometry + hit-test + round-trips); 78 tests pass.

v2.105.0  (shaders, cont.: triplanar + scrolling UV)
  - Triplanar mapping: project a texture along the three world axes and blend by the
    surface normal, so terrain, cliffs and arbitrary meshes texture with NO UV seams
    or stretching. Tiling is the world-space scale.
  - Scrolling UV: animate a texture by a per-second UV speed (flowing water, lava,
    conveyor belts, scrolling skies) — driven by elapsed time, applied to every
    textured path.
  - Both exposed in the Mesh Renderer inspector, saved on .okaymat materials and in
    scenes (back-compatible). shaders suite extended (round-trips + render checks that
    scrolling changes over time and triplanar differs from UV mapping); 77 tests pass.

v2.104.0  (shaders, cont.: rim light + outline)
  - Per-material Rim (Fresnel) backlight: a colored glow that strengthens toward
    grazing angles (strength, power, color). Works with any shader and auto-uses the
    per-pixel path; pairs beautifully with Toon for a stylized edge glow.
  - Silhouette Outline shader (inverted hull): renders an expanded shell of the mesh
    in a solid color just behind it, leaving a clean cartoon edge (width + color). The
    shell is pushed slightly back so it only shows at the silhouette, never occluding
    the surface — verified it adds an outline ring with zero pixels lost.
  - Both are exposed in the Mesh Renderer inspector, stored on .okaymat materials and
    in scenes (back-compatible). shaders test suite extended (rim/outline round-trips
    + a render check that the outline adds a silhouette ring); 77 tests pass.

v2.103.0  (shaders: surface-model selector + Toon)
  - Materials/MeshRenderers now pick a Shader (surface model), like Unity: Standard
    (the full lit PBR-ish model), Unlit (flat base color, no lighting), or Toon.
  - New Toon (cel) shader: quantizes the diffuse lighting into a configurable number
    of hard bands (Cel Bands, 2-6) and collapses the specular into a single hard
    glint, for a cartoon look. It auto-uses the per-pixel path so it works out of the
    box regardless of the global Per-Pixel Lighting toggle.
  - Inspector Shader dropdown (folds the old Unlit flag into the selector) + Cel Bands
    slider; the look is stored on .okaymat materials and in the scene (back-compatible).
  - New shaders test suite (preset + scene round-trips, and a render check that Toon
    posterizes a lit sphere into far fewer shades than Standard); 77 tests pass.

v2.102.0  (Cinemachine, cont.: dolly track)
  - New DollyPath component: a smooth camera rail (Cinemachine's Path/SmoothPath). An
    ordered list of waypoints evaluated as a Catmull-Rom spline, optionally looped.
  - Virtual cameras gained a Tracked Dolly body: constrain the camera to a DollyPath
    while the aim still tracks LookAt/Follow — glide a curved track past a subject.
    Auto-Dolly slides to the path point nearest the Follow target.
  - New DollyCart component: drive any GameObject (camera, platform) along a path at a
    speed — on-rails shots and moving geometry.
  - Inspector UI (editable waypoint list, add/remove), Add Component entries, and
    back-compatible serialization. Cinemachine tests extended (spline endpoints,
    auto-dolly tracking, cart travel); 76 tests pass.

v2.101.0  (Cinemachine, cont.: FreeLook orbital rig)
  - Virtual cameras gained a FreeLook mode (Cinemachine's orbital rig): orbit the
    Follow target on a sphere by yaw/pitch at a fixed radius, with a pivot height and
    pitch clamp. When on, it overrides the body offset and aim — the camera always
    frames the target. Ideal for 3rd-person player cameras.
  - Optional mouse-look drives yaw/pitch (hold a configurable button, with adjustable
    sensitivity), or set the angles from a script/controller.
  - Full Inspector UI and back-compatible serialization. Cinemachine test suite
    extended (orbit stays on the sphere as yaw sweeps); 76 tests pass.

v2.100.0  (Cinemachine, cont.: binding modes, composer, impulse)
  - Virtual cameras gained a Binding Mode: World Space (fixed framing) or Lock To
    Target — an orbital/3rd-person chase cam whose offset rotates with the target's
    facing, so it stays behind as the target turns.
  - Aim got a Look Offset (Tracked Object Offset — aim at the head, not the feet) and
    a Dead Zone (degrees): the camera holds its aim until the target drifts beyond the
    angle, removing micro-jitter (a simplified Composer dead zone).
  - Added a one-shot Impulse system: VirtualCamera::AddImpulse(strength) fires a
    decaying camera shake on an event (explosion, landing, hit), with per-vcam Impulse
    Decay. An Inspector "Test Impulse" button previews it.
  - CinemachineBrain blends now ease-in/ease-out by default (toggle) for softer cuts.
  - All new fields serialize (back-compatible) and have Inspector controls. 76 tests
    pass (cinemachine suite extended: orbital binding, impulse settle).

v2.99.0  (Cinemachine-style virtual cameras)
  - New VirtualCamera component (Cinemachine-style): describes a *shot* — a Follow
    target + offset (the body), a LookAt target (the aim), a lens (field of view),
    position/rotation damping, and optional handheld noise (shake). It never renders.
  - New CinemachineBrain component (lives on the main camera): each frame it solves
    every virtual camera, makes the highest-priority enabled one "live", and eases the
    real camera's pose + FOV toward it over a Blend Time when the live shot changes.
  - Author several vcams and cut/blend between them just by changing priorities —
    chase cams, cutscene framing, screen shake, etc.
  - Full Inspector UI, GameObject > Create Virtual Camera, Add Component entries, and
    scene serialization. New test suite (cinemachine); 76 tests pass.

v2.98.0  (camera: layers, culling mask, physical camera)
  - Added Unity-style named Layers (32 layers; GameObject.layer). Each GameObject now
    sits on a layer, editable from a new Layer dropdown in the Inspector header.
  - Cameras gained a Culling Mask (Camera.cullingMask): a per-layer set of what the
    camera renders, with an Everything/Nothing + per-layer Inspector control. Meshes on
    unselected layers are skipped — useful for mini-maps, helper geometry, layered passes.
  - Cameras gained a Physical Camera mode (focal length + sensor height) that derives
    the field of view from real lens optics, matching Unity's physical camera. The
    Inspector shows the effective FOV. New Camera::VerticalFovDegrees() helper.
  - layer, cullingMask and the physical-camera fields are all saved with the scene.

v2.97.0  (optimization: less per-frame allocation)
  - Scene::FindObjectsOfType (called many times per frame by physics, rendering and
    UI) used to allocate a temporary vector for EVERY game object via GetComponents.
    It now collects matches directly into one reserved output vector (new
    GameObject::CollectComponents), cutting per-frame heap allocations from O(objects)
    to one per query. Identical results; 75 tests pass.

v2.96.0  (physics optimization)
  - Faster collision broadphase (2D + 3D). Each collider's world AABB was being
    recomputed O(n) times per frame inside the all-pairs loop; it's now computed
    once per step. The end-of-contact cleanup did an O(contacts x colliders) linear
    "still alive?" scan; it now uses a hash set (O(1) lookup). ~3.8x faster physics
    step with 200 colliders; scales much better with collider count. 75 tests pass.

v2.95.0  (renderer optimization, cont.)
  - Extended the incremental-barycentric fast path to the remaining rasterizers
    (flat/unlit and per-pixel/Phong), so every triangle path now advances the edge
    functions by a constant per pixel instead of recomputing them. Unlit meshes and
    per-pixel-lit scenes get the same fill-rate win. Output identical; 75 tests pass.

v2.94.0  (renderer optimization)
  - Faster software rasterizer. The lit and textured triangle paths now use
    INCREMENTAL barycentric coordinates (computed once per scanline, then advanced
    by a constant per pixel) instead of recomputing them from scratch at every
    pixel — removing the per-pixel edge-function multiplies that dominated fill
    cost on big surfaces (the ground). Also skip per-pixel fog and specular
    interpolation entirely when a triangle has neither (the common case). ~15-20%
    faster fill on typical 3D scenes; identical output (75 tests pass).

v2.93.0
  - 3D anti-aliasing (2x supersampling) is ON by default again. With the v2.92
    W-buffer depth fix removing the z-fighting, the only thing left was faint edge
    shimmer during motion; 2x AA clears it. Auto-performance still drops to 1x on
    heavy scenes / large viewports so it won't tank FPS. Toggle in View menu.

v2.92.0  (camera depth-precision fix + more camera options)
  - DEPTH BUFFER is now a W-buffer (stores 1/w) instead of z/w. The old z/w depth
    had terrible precision far from a small near plane, so surfaces z-fought and
    flickered during motion -- which is why raising the near clip "fixed" third
    person but broke first person (it clipped nearby geometry). 1/w has uniform
    precision, so a small Unity-like near clip (0.3) now works everywhere with no
    z-fighting -- in first person, third person, and the scene view. (Occlusion
    verified; 75 tests pass.)
  - Camera: added FOV Axis (Vertical/Horizontal, like Unity) and a Viewport Rect
    (Camera.rect: normalized x/y/w/h for split-screen, mini-maps, picture-in-
    picture) -- both in the Camera inspector and saved with the scene.
  - Camera defaults to Perspective (Unity-like); near clip back to 0.3.

v2.91.0  (camera + gizmo fixes)
  - Editor grid/gizmo/selection lines now clip to the true near plane (z+w>=0)
    like the mesh, instead of the camera plane (w>0.05). Before, a line endpoint
    just in front of the camera projected with an exploding 1/w, so ground lines
    snapped/swung while zooming.
  - Camera component now defaults to Perspective (matching Unity; 2D scenes still
    set Orthographic). Near clip back to a Unity-like 0.3.
  - Scene view near clip is adjustable (Stats > Rendering > Debug view).

v2.91.0  (bug-hunt batch)
  - Fixed glitchy grid/gizmo lines on the ground while zooming. The editor's line
    clipper (added when the selection box was made to hug geometry) clipped at the
    camera plane (w > 0.05) instead of the true near plane, so a line endpoint just
    in front of the camera projected with an exploding 1/w and the lines SNAPPED/
    swung as you zoomed -- a regression that began when those ground lines were
    fixed. Now clips at the real near plane (z + w >= 0) like the mesh, so grid and
    selection lines stay put and move smoothly during zoom. (75 tests pass.)

v2.90.0  (bug-hunt batch)
  - Fixed the 3D "glitchy zoom" on high-DPI / Windows-scaled displays. The window
    is high-DPI aware (renderer scales by the framebuffer scale), but the 3D view
    was rendered at LOGICAL resolution and then linearly UPSCALED to physical
    pixels -- so the image was soft and, crucially, the whole surface shimmered/
    crawled while the camera moved (the resample shifts sub-pixel each frame). It
    didn't show in a still. Real engines render at native pixels; now this one does
    too: the 3D view renders at the true physical resolution and maps 1:1 to the
    screen -- sharp and stable in motion. (No-op on non-scaled displays.)
  - Reverted v2.89's "render at half-res while the camera moves" (it traded sharp
    motion for FPS; that softness during motion was itself a zoom artifact).

v2.89.0  (bug-hunt batch)
  - Fixed the real "glitch when zooming in" -- it was FRAME-RATE STUTTER, not a
    rendering artifact. Zoomed in, a big surface (the ground) fills the whole view,
    so the software renderer's per-pixel fill cost spikes and FPS drops, making
    camera motion choppy. Two changes:
      * Dynamic resolution: while the Scene camera is moving the 3D view renders at
        half resolution (and no supersampling) for smooth panning/zooming, then
        snaps back to full resolution the instant the camera settles.
      * Reverted 2x anti-aliasing back to OFF by default (v2.88 turned it on, which
        quadrupled fill cost and made the stutter worse). Still available in
        View > "3D Anti-aliasing (2x)" if you have the headroom.

v2.88.0  (bug-hunt batch)
  - 3D anti-aliasing (2x supersampling) is now ON by default. It was off, so 3D
    edges were hard 1-pixel stair-steps that CRAWL/shimmer while the camera moves
    and look fine when it stops -- the "glitch only while zooming/moving" report.
    Auto-performance still drops it to 1x on heavy scenes, and now also on very
    large viewports (keeps the supersampled buffer within a pixel budget so 2x AA
    never tanks FPS). Toggle: View menu > "3D Anti-aliasing (2x)".

v2.87.0  (bug-hunt batch)
  - Fixed specular highlights computed PER-TRIANGLE: a single Blinn-Phong value
    from the triangle centroid made a hard diagonal highlight seam on big quads
    (e.g. a shiny ground). Specular is now per-vertex and interpolated across the
    triangle in the smooth/textured paths, so highlights are smooth. Verified
    before/after on a shiny ground. (75 tests pass.)

v2.86.0  (bug-hunt batch)
  - Fixed distance fog computed PER-TRIANGLE: each triangle got one fog value from
    its centroid distance, so a big 2-triangle quad (e.g. the ground) showed a hard
    DIAGONAL two-tone seam when fog was on. Fog is now a per-vertex factor
    interpolated across the triangle (smooth/textured/per-pixel paths), so fog
    fades smoothly with distance and the seam is gone. Verified before/after on a
    fogged ground. (75 tests pass.)

v2.85.0  (bug-hunt batch)
  - Fixed normals under non-uniform scale: the renderer transformed normals by the
    model matrix directly, which skews them when scale isn't uniform (e.g. a ground
    scaled {40,1,40} or a stretched sphere) -> wrong lighting that "acts up" the
    more the scale departs from uniform. Now uses the inverse-transpose (cofactor)
    basis, correct for any scale/shear. Verified on a 3:1 stretched, specular sphere.
  - Fixed Mat4::LookAt looking straight up/down: when the view direction was
    parallel to the up vector (e.g. a top-down camera aimed straight down with
    up = +Y), the basis collapsed and the view rendered black/garbled. Now falls
    back to an alternate up axis so straight up/down cameras work. (75 tests pass.)

v2.84.0
  - Fixed a real near-plane clipping bug: triangles were clipped on w > 1e-4
    (the camera plane) instead of the true near plane (z_clip >= -w_clip). That
    left geometry BETWEEN the camera and the near plane in the pipeline, where
    1/w explodes (~1e4x) and projects vertices to gigantic screen coordinates —
    precision breaks down and surfaces can glitch when you zoom in very close.
    Now clips on the actual near plane, so close-up geometry stays stable and
    every drawn fragment's depth is inside [-1,1]. (75 tests pass; verified no
    regression across normal, grazing, and camera-in-plane views.)
  - New debug tool: Stats > Rendering > Debug view > "Save Scene view -> PNG".
    Writes the EXACT pixels the engine renders for the current Scene view to
    scene_capture.png, bypassing the screen/monitor. Lets a glitch be captured
    as true engine output (rules out camera-photo moire / display artifacts).

v2.83.0
  - Scene view: on-screen Camera HUD (top-left) showing the orbit camera's zoom
    distance, yaw/pitch angle, eye position, target, and FOV. A screenshot now
    records exactly where you're looking from, so visual issues can be reproduced
    at the same camera. Toggle in Stats > Rendering > Debug view ("Camera HUD").

v2.82.0
  - Reverted the 3D ground back to a SOLID block (it was briefly a flat plane with
    the collider offset below it, which looked like a thin sheet floating over a
    wireframe cage once the gizmo box drew fully). Now the visible ground and its
    collider are the same solid shape again — no box hanging underneath.
  - New "Debug view" tools in Stats > Rendering (Scene view only, never affect the
    Game view or a build; all persist):
      * Ground grid on/off
      * Sky gradient on/off (turn off to see meshes against a flat background and
        tell geometry apart from the sky behind it)
      * Colliders on/off, Gizmos on/off
      * Wireframe (all meshes) — draw every mesh as edges
      * World axes at the origin (X red, Y green, Z blue)
      * Scene camera FOV slider (20-110 deg) — lower = flatter/less perspective
        distortion, higher = wider/more edge stretch

v2.81.0
  - Fixed: the yellow selection box (and the grid / camera / light gizmos) didn't
    match the geometry — the rendered ground would spill past the yellow outline.
    Cause: the mesh renderer near-plane-clips its triangles, but the editor's gizmo
    lines simply DROPPED any endpoint at/behind the camera. So when the camera sat
    within a large ground, the near edges of the box vanished and only the far peak
    was drawn, while the green mesh still filled the screen. Gizmo lines are now
    clipped to the near plane like the mesh, so the yellow box traces the actual
    ground shape from every angle.
  - Stats > Rendering: the effects list now has a clear "Effects" heading and an
    accurate note (Hemisphere ambient, Tone mapping, and Anti-aliasing are ON by
    default; the rest are opt-in). All 9 are toggleable here.

v2.80.0
  - The 3D ground is now a flat PLANE instead of a 1-unit-thick cube slab. The old
    slab had vertical side walls; when the follow camera dropped near an edge you
    saw that wall edge-on as a dark wedge, which read as "the floor isn't square /
    there's a glitch at the edge." A plane has no sides, so the floor is just a flat
    square surface from every angle. Same 40×40 footprint (24×24 for 3D Platformer) —
    NOT enlarged. The collider is unchanged: a thin box whose top is still flush at
    y=0 (offset down by 0.5), so the player stands and falls exactly as before.
  - Follow cameras (third-person and click-to-move) now clamp their height so they
    never sink to or below the floor. ThirdPerson allowed pitching down to -20deg,
    which at close range could drop the camera under the ground — from beneath a
    floor you'd see it edge-on (a glitchy "wedge") or, with the one-sided plane, not
    at all. The camera is now kept above the player's base / a small floor clearance.
  - The ground plane is marked double-sided, so it stays visible even if you orbit
    the editor Scene camera below it (a single-sided plane would otherwise vanish).

v2.79.0
  - Added a "Gizmos" toggle (View menu): hide the editor-only Scene overlays — the
    yellow selection bounding box, camera frustum, and light icons — for a clean
    view. (These never appeared in the Game view or a build; the toggle just lets
    you confirm that and declutter the Scene.) Saved across sessions.

v2.78.0
  - No more facet lines with per-pixel lighting off: lit untextured meshes now shade
    via the smooth (Gouraud) path using the face normal interpolated per vertex, so
    a flat floor has no hard line on its triangle diagonal under any light — while
    cubes keep their crisp hard edges. Combined with per-pixel + rim off (v2.77),
    the ground is clean at any zoom: no moiré, no cracks, no facet lines.

v2.77.0
  - Per-pixel lighting is now OFF by default. It produced a moiré/shimmer on large
    flat surfaces (e.g. the ground) when the camera zoomed in close; flat/Gouraud
    shading is clean there (and the rasterizer crack fixes from v2.75 apply to it
    too). Re-enable it any time in the Rendering panel.
  - Rim light is now OFF by default. Its grazing-angle glow swept across big floors
    (brightest at the far corners) as the camera moved. Also opt-in in the panel.
  - (Reverted the v2.76 template fog — it was based on a wrong diagnosis; the real
    cause was the rim/per-pixel shading above.)

v2.76.0
  - 3D templates: the ground now fades into the horizon with distance fog, so you
    no longer see a hard edge / gray sky wedge past the end of the floor when you
    roam (the floor size is unchanged). Fog is color-matched to the sky horizon for
    a seamless, endless-looking ground. Tunable in Scene render settings.

v2.75.0
  - Fixed the big-ground rendering glitches: torn/jagged silhouette when zoomed in,
    dashed edge cracks, and faint depth banding across large surfaces. Root cause
    was float precision collapsing in the per-pixel triangle math on very large
    (screen-filling) triangles; the rasterizer now computes coverage in double
    precision, so huge floors render clean at any zoom/angle. Affects all four
    rasterizers; verified with an offscreen render probe.

v2.74.0
  - Every new scene now starts with a Main Camera (including the Empty template),
    so a fresh scene renders and Plays right away. The 2D/3D starters reuse it
    (set to Orthographic / Perspective) instead of adding a second camera.

v2.73.0
  - Rendering: added a Color Grading post pass (Rendering panel) — Brightness,
    Contrast, Saturation, Vignette and Gamma. Neutral/off by default, so existing
    scenes look the same until you dial it in.

v2.72.0
  - Fixed 3D face flickering (z-fighting): the depth test now uses a small bias so
    the first-drawn surface wins near-ties, stopping the shimmer between abutting
    faces (very visible on the blocky character's overlapping body parts).
  - Click-To-Move now has a RuneScape-style follow camera built in: it trails the
    player and keeps it centered. Middle-drag rotates the camera, the wheel zooms,
    and optional keys rotate it. Tunable in the inspector and saved with the scene.

v2.71.0
  - New: Click-To-Move controller (RuneScape / Diablo style) + a "Point & Click"
    template. Click the ground and your Character walks there (unprojected onto the
    ground plane via the main camera), turning to face travel with walk/run anim.
    Full inspector + saved with the scene.
  - Fixed: characters not falling off platforms. The 3D starter grounds had a
    double-scaled box collider (Transform scale AND collider size both 40), making
    the collider ~40x bigger than the visible floor, so you never left it. Grounds
    now use a unit collider sized by the Transform.
  - Fixed: third-person view had a slanted/rolled horizon at some angles — the
    camera rotation is now built directly from yaw/pitch (no roll), so the horizon
    stays level.

v2.70.0
  - Third-Person camera: corrected vertical look so mouse-up looks up (only the
    pitch axis was reversed after v2.69; horizontal was already right).

v2.69.0
  - Third-Person camera: flipped both mouse-look axes to the expected orbit
    direction (the prior default felt inverted both ways). Use the Invert X / Invert
    Y toggles in the controller's inspector if you prefer the opposite.

v2.68.0
  - Colliders auto-fit the object. Adding a Box/Circle/Capsule 2D collider now
    sizes it to the SpriteRenderer, and a Box/Sphere/Capsule 3D collider to the
    MeshRenderer bounds — automatically, on add. Each collider also has a "Fit to
    Object" button and an "Auto Fit" toggle that keeps it matched to the renderer
    every frame (great for objects that change size at runtime). Saved with the scene.
  - Third-Person camera: fixed the inverted vertical look — mouse-up now looks up
    (matching first-person). Horizontal was already correct; pitch was reversed.

v2.67.0
  - Character Controller 2D — greatly expanded with real platformer game-feel:
    run/sprint key, acceleration & deceleration, gamepad stick support, sprite
    facing flip, and (top-down) diagonal normalization. Platformer adds multi-jump
    (double jump), variable/hold-for-higher jumps, coyote time, jump buffering, air
    control, a fall-speed clamp and extra fall gravity. Grounded state now comes
    from real collisions (correct on slopes/platforms) with the old velocity
    heuristic kept as a fallback. All new fields are in the inspector and saved.
  - 2D sprites — resize handles in the Scene view: select a sprite and drag the 8
    corner/edge handles to change its size, the same affordance UI widgets have
    (you previously could only nudge it with the uniform-scale gizmo).
  - 2D sprites — Sort Order now works in the editor preview: sprites are drawn
    back-to-front by Sort Order (matching the built game) instead of scene order.

v2.66.0
  - Scripting — many more event handlers. Physics now has distinct enter/stay/exit
    for both triggers and collisions: on_trigger_enter/stay/exit and
    on_collision_enter/stay/exit (work for 2D and 3D). New pointer/mouse events:
    on_mouse_enter, on_mouse_exit, on_mouse_over, on_mouse_down, on_mouse_up, and
    on_click (a full press+release on the object). The cursor is picked against an
    object's sprite/2D-collider bounds via the main camera. Older on_trigger() and
    on_collision() still work as the "enter" handlers.
  - Visual scripting — new triggers: On Trigger Enter/Exit and On Mouse
    Enter/Exit/Down/Up/Over (alongside the existing On Collision / On Click). New
    instructions: if_goto (branch on a variable), call (run a script event on this
    object), send_to (message one named object), set_rotation / set_rotation3,
    velocity3 / impulse3 / force3 (3D rigidbody), set_sprite, and set_visible.

v2.65.0
  - UI text z-order (fixed): screen-space text was drawn before UI panels, so a
    label placed on a panel was hidden behind it with no way to bring it forward.
    Screen-space text now draws on top of panels/images/controls (below only
    dropdown popups and tooltips), in both the editor and the built game.
  - 2D Platformer template (fixed): the player had a physics body but no movement
    script, so it just sat there. It now has a CharacterController2D in Platformer
    mode — move left/right and jump (Space/W) with no scripting needed.
  - New Project: removed the "3D Platformer" template. Coin Collector and Snake now
    live in their own "Mini games" category instead of under 2D templates.

v2.64.0
  - Skybox in the built game (fixed): the player drew the sky with a draw call that
    silently does nothing on some GPUs/drivers, leaving the game showing a black sky
    even though the editor previewed it correctly. It's now painted as plain
    horizontal color strips that render everywhere. The player also honors the
    camera's "Solid Color" clear flag (skybox suppressed) just like the editor.
  - Third-Person Controller (fixed + expanded): the camera look was inverted (mouse
    moved the view the wrong way vs. first-person) and pitch placed the camera BELOW
    the player looking up. Look now matches first-person, and a positive pitch lifts
    the camera so it looks DOWN. New options: Invert X/Y, min/max distance, zoom
    speed, min/max pitch, over-the-shoulder Shoulder Offset, follow Damping
    (smoothing), and Body Facing mode (Face Movement vs. Face Camera for aim/shooter
    feel). Left-stick now drives movement too. All saved with the scene.
  - UI: children parented under another UI widget now follow their parent (Unity
    rect-in-rect) — move/resize a panel and its children move with it.
  - UI Drop Target: drop actions — a dragged item's script gets on_drag_start/
    on_drag/on_drop; a slot gets on_drag_enter/on_drag_exit/on_receive/on_remove, so
    you can trigger functions when something is dropped on or dragged off a slot.
  - New Project window is now resizable.

v2.63.0
  - New Project: redesigned the chooser — categorized template cards (color-coded
    by type) with selection, a description panel, and a clear Create/Cancel footer.
  - UI Drop Target: much more customization — a slot background (color, rounded
    corners, border), separate accept vs reject hover tints (reject shows when a
    wrong-tag item hovers), and snap-dropped-item-to-center. Drawn in both the
    editor and the game; all saved with the scene.

v2.62.0
  - Updater (fixed): the core bug was that the version baked into the editor lagged
    one release behind VERSION.txt — the build was stamped BEFORE the version bump —
    so after updating, the app still reported the old version and kept offering the
    update (looked like it "never updated"). Builds are now stamped correctly.
    Also hardened the download/swap: (1) downloads are pinned to the latest commit's
    SHA (an immutable URL the CDN can't serve stale), and (2) on Windows the swap is
    handed to a small batch helper that waits for the editor to close, replaces the
    exe, and relaunches — instead of renaming a running exe (which antivirus/file
    locks could block). NOTE: install 2.62 directly once; updates from 2.62 work.

v2.61.0
  - Canvas: Visible toggle + master Opacity. "Visible" hides/shows every widget
    under a canvas at once (works in the editor and the game). "Opacity" is a 0..1
    master fade applied to the canvas's widgets in the built game (text, images,
    panels, buttons, sliders, toggles, progress bars, scroll views) — drive it at
    runtime for menu fade in/out. Both are in the Canvas inspector and saved with
    the scene.

v2.60.0
  - Canvas: more options. The Scale Factor is now a universal UI zoom (it applies
    in Scale-With-Screen mode too, not just Constant Pixel Size). The inspector
    gained reference-resolution presets (1080p/720p/1440p + portrait), Match
    Width/Height preset buttons, tooltips, and a live "current scale" readout.

v2.59.0
  - Change: the Play/Stop shortcut moved from Space to Ctrl+P, so Space is free
    to jump while the game is running (pressing Space used to stop the game).

v2.58.0
  - Performance: per-pixel shading micro-optimizations. Global render-state
    accessors (rim/shadow/sky toggles) are hoisted out of the inner loop, the rim
    term uses cheap integer powers instead of pow(), and the diffuse/specular
    lighting skip a redundant per-pixel normal re-normalization (the renderer
    already passes a unit normal). ~10-15% faster geometry on a basic scene.

v2.57.0
  - Fix: dotted "crack" lines across the ground (and other large quads). Pixels on
    a shared triangle edge could fall outside both triangles from float rounding;
    the rasterizer now accepts a sub-pixel bias so adjacent triangles overlap and
    the seam is gone.
  - Fix: the Game preview now ignores the first-person player's body in edit mode
    too (it used to only take effect after pressing Play).
  - Templates: new Third Person template (your Character + orbit camera, WASD +
    jump + walk/run animation — you see and control the character).
  - Templates: 3D Platformer now uses the Character (was a plain cube) with a
    fitted collider and a follow camera.
  - Templates: player colliders are offset to wrap the body (feet->head) where the
    player is a Character.

v2.56.0
  - Fix: the camera frustum gizmo in the Scene view pointed the opposite way to the
    actual view. Cameras look down -Z, but the gizmo was drawn along +Z; it now
    points where the camera actually looks.
  - Fix: the FPS template player's collider sat too low (centered on the feet). It
    now has an offset so the box wraps the body from feet to head. Existing scenes:
    set the collider's Offset Y (or use Fit) in the inspector.

v2.55.0
  - Fix: walk/run animations no longer play backwards. The character was modelled
    facing +Z while the cameras/controllers use -Z as forward, so the body walked
    backwards relative to its facing (moonwalk). The character mesh now faces -Z to
    match, and the third-person controller aims accordingly. Walking forward now
    animates forward (and vice-versa) in both first- and third-person.

v2.54.0
  - Fix: first-person no longer makes your character vanish. Instead of hiding the
    body mesh globally, the first-person camera now simply IGNORES the player's own
    body — so it still shows in the Scene view, casts shadows, and renders for any
    other camera; only the eye-camera skips drawing it. (New Camera.ignoreObject.)

v2.53.0
  - Performance (defaults): shadows, SSAO and bloom are now OFF by default — they
    were the bulk of the frame cost. A basic scene roughly doubled in FPS. They're
    all one click away in the Stats window's "Rendering (Lighting & Post FX)"
    panel for when you want them.
  - Performance: added a Render Scale slider (same panel). The 3D view renders at
    that fraction of resolution and upscales — the single biggest FPS lever for
    the software renderer (0.75x ~ +75% fps, 0.5x ~ 2x+), at the cost of sharpness.
  - Fix: deleting the main camera then adding a new one no longer leaves the Game
    tab black — the editor re-detects a live camera instead of using a stale one.
  - Fix: Space now jumps in the Game tab instead of pausing (ImGui keyboard nav is
    disabled while the game runs so input reaches the game).
  - Note: the grazing-angle "zoom" speckle was directional-shadow acne; with
    shadows off by default it's gone (bias also improved for when shadows are on).

v2.52.0
  - Fix: the skybox no longer disappears in 3D scenes. The tone-map and bloom
    post passes were forcing every pixel opaque, so the transparent background
    (where the skybox shows through) turned solid black. They now preserve the
    background alpha.
  - Performance: the mesh rasterizer is now multithreaded — the frame is split
    into horizontal bands across CPU cores, each filling its own scanlines (no
    locks). On top of v2.51's post-FX threading this roughly halves frame time
    again on a 4-core machine (e.g. a busy scene ~103 ms -> ~65 ms).
  - Fix: SSAO grain. The half-res AO pass got its blur back (3x3 at half res), so
    contact shadows are smooth instead of speckled.
  - Fix: reduced directional-shadow acne (larger normal-offset + depth bias).

v2.51.0
  - Performance (major): the 3D renderer was far too slow with the full effect
    stack on (a simple scene could sit at ~1-3 fps). Fixes:
      * Native-resolution render by default instead of 2x supersampling (4x fewer
        pixels; FXAA still smooths edges). Editor "3D AA" and the player now
        default to 1x.
      * Bloom now runs at quarter resolution (downsample -> blur -> bilinear
        upsample) instead of a full-res multi-pass blur (~10x faster bloom).
      * SSAO now computes at half resolution and uses 8 samples (~4-5x faster).
      * The full-screen post passes (SSAO, bloom, tone map, FXAA) are now
        multithreaded across CPU cores.
      * Shadow PCF reduced from 12 to 6 disk taps (per shaded pixel).
    Net: a heavy test scene went from ~1540 ms/frame to ~100 ms/frame on a 4-core
    machine (~15x), and lighter scenes are well within real-time. All effects stay
    on by default and remain individually toggleable.

v2.50.0
  - Rendering: smooth-shaded curved primitives. Sphere, Icosphere, Torus and
    Capsule now ship with generated smooth per-vertex normals, so they render as
    genuinely round surfaces instead of showing flat triangle facets. Combined
    with per-pixel lighting this removes the visible faceting on curved meshes.

v2.49.0
  - Rendering: metallic materials. MeshRenderer gained a Metallic property. Metals
    lose their diffuse term and tint both their specular highlight and their
    environment (sky) reflection by the albedo color, so gold reflects gold and
    chrome mirrors the sky, while dielectrics keep a white highlight. Metals also
    reflect strongly without needing an explicit reflectivity. Exposed in the
    inspector and saved with the scene.

v2.48.0
  - Rendering: colored, multi-light specular. Highlights used to come only from
    the directional sun and were always white. The per-pixel path now accumulates
    Blinn-Phong specular from every light (directional + point + spot), each tinted
    by its own color and distance/cone attenuation, with the sun's contribution
    shadowed. So a red point light gives a red highlight, a blue one a blue
    highlight, and point/spot lights produce highlights at all (they didn't before).

v2.47.0
  - Rendering (important fix): per-pixel features now work on meshes without
    explicit normals. The per-pixel path was gated behind HasNormals(), so the
    built-in primitives (Cube, Plane, Cylinder, ...) and the flat-shaded Character
    silently fell back to a basic path with NO cast shadows, reflections, rim
    light or per-pixel hemisphere ambient. They now go through the per-pixel path
    using the face normal (so they stay crisply flat-shaded) and receive all of
    those — e.g. a plain cube on a plane finally casts a real soft shadow.

v2.46.0
  - Rendering: hemisphere ambient light. The ambient term is no longer one flat
    color — up-facing surfaces pick up the sky color and down-facing ones pick up
    the ground color (from the scene's sky gradient), with the horizontal midpoint
    kept at the previous ambient level so overall brightness is unchanged. Shaded
    sides and undersides now read as three-dimensional instead of dead-flat. On by
    default (HemisphereAmbient / HemisphereStrength), with an editor toggle.

v2.45.0
  - Rendering: specular/gloss maps. MeshRenderer gained a Specular Map slot — a
    grayscale texture whose per-texel luminance scales the specular highlight and
    the environment reflection, so one material can blend shiny and matte regions
    (per-pixel lighting). Exposed in the inspector and saved with the scene.
  - Build/fix: builds now bundle the normal map and specular map alongside the
    base texture (previously normal maps were referenced but not copied into the
    shipped game folder).

v2.44.0
  - Rendering: environment reflections. Materials gained a Reflectivity property
    (per-pixel path): the surface mirrors the scene's sky gradient about its
    normal, blended in with a Fresnel (Schlick) weight so edges reflect more than
    face-on — a cheap glossy/metal look that responds to the normal map. Tunable
    in the MeshRenderer inspector; saved with the scene.

v2.43.0
  - Rendering: Fresnel rim light. The per-pixel path adds a soft (1 - n·view)^p
    glow at grazing angles, tinted by the lit color, which separates objects from
    the background and reads as a subtle backlight. On by default (RimLightEnabled
    / RimStrength / RimPower).
  - Editor: new "Rendering (Lighting & Post FX)" panel in the Stats window with
    live toggles + sliders for per-pixel lighting, shadows (softness), SSAO
    (radius/strength), rim light, bloom (threshold/intensity), tone mapping
    (exposure) and FXAA — so the whole pipeline is tunable without recompiling.

v2.42.0
  - Rendering: softer shadows. The shadow filter moved from a rigid 3x3 box to a
    12-tap Poisson-disk PCF, giving smoother, less-banded penumbrae for the same
    sample count. Edge softness is tunable (ShadowSoftness, in shadow-map texels).

v2.41.0
  - Rendering: filmic tone mapping (ACES) + exposure. Instead of clamping bright
    pixels to a flat white, highlights now roll off along a filmic S-curve so
    speculars, emissives and bloom keep their hue and gradient; shadows/midtones
    gain a little contrast. Exposure scales the whole frame. Runs after bloom and
    before AA. On by default (ToneMapEnabled / Exposure toggles).

v2.40.0
  - Rendering: bloom (bright-pass glow). Pixels brighter than a threshold are
    extracted, blurred (separable box, 3 passes ~ gaussian, resolution-scaled
    radius) and added back, so highlights, light sources and emissive surfaces
    bleed a soft halo. On by default (BloomEnabled / BloomThreshold /
    BloomIntensity toggles), runs after SSAO and before FXAA.

v2.39.0
  - Rendering: anti-aliasing (FXAA post-process). Edges are detected by local
    contrast and softened, smoothing the jaggies on geometry/shadow silhouettes
    without the cost of full supersampling. On by default (FXAAEnabled toggle).

v2.38.0
  - Rendering: screen-space ambient occlusion (SSAO). A per-pixel position/normal
    G-buffer drives a hemisphere occlusion pass that softly darkens creases,
    contacts and corners, adding depth that flat lighting misses. Tunable
    (SSAORadius/SSAOStrength), blurred, on by default.
  - First/Third Person Controllers: fixed inverted look + backwards movement.
    Forward now follows where the camera looks (its -Z), mouse-right turns right,
    and the third-person camera sits behind the player looking at it. Added an
    Invert Y option to the first-person controller.

v2.37.0
  - Rendering: real-time shadows. A depth pre-pass from the directional light
    builds a 1024px shadow map; per-pixel shading projects each fragment into it
    and darkens fragments the light can't see, so objects cast soft (3x3 PCF)
    shadows onto each other and the ground. Normal-offset bias avoids self-
    shadowing acne. On by default (ShadowsEnabled() toggle).
  - Rendering: per-pixel world position/normal interpolation is now perspective-
    correct, fixing point-light and shadow sampling on large triangles.

v2.36.0
  - Rendering: normal (bump) maps. MeshRenderer has a Normal Map slot (+ Bump
    Strength); the per-pixel lighting path builds a per-triangle tangent frame,
    samples the map and perturbs the surface normal, so flat geometry shows
    convincing bumps/detail that react to light. Saves/loads with the scene and
    has an inspector field (drag a PNG from the Project panel).

v2.35.0
  - Rendering: mipmapping. Each texture now builds a downsampled pyramid, and the
    renderer picks the right level PER PIXEL from the texture's screen-space
    density (trilinear-filtered between levels). Distant / grazing-angle textures
    are smooth instead of a shimmering moire mess, while up-close detail stays
    sharp. Applies to both the textured and per-pixel-lit paths.

v2.34.0
  - Merged the rendering and scripting lineages into a single engine release.

v2.33.0
  - Rendering: per-pixel (Phong) lighting. Lit meshes are now shaded at every
    pixel using the interpolated surface normal, instead of shading per-vertex
    and smearing the result across each triangle. The payoff: low-poly and
    curved surfaces look smooth, and specular highlights are clean and round
    instead of faceted/blocky. Works for textured and untextured meshes.
    On by default; falls back to faster per-vertex shading if turned off.

v2.32.0
  - Rendering: better 3D model + texture quality.
    - Textured meshes are now lit SMOOTHLY (per-vertex/Gouraud) instead of one
      flat shade per face — textures get proper soft shading and specular
      highlights across each surface.
    - Bilinear texture filtering: textures are smooth instead of blocky /
      pixelated (both regular textures and matcap/lit-sphere sampling).
    - MeshRenderer gained an `enabled` flag to hide a single mesh without
      deactivating the object.
  - First person fix: the First Person Controller now hides your own character's
    body while playing (you look out through its eyes — no more seeing/clipping
    your own model). It stays visible in the editor. The First Person template's
    camera is centered at eye height to match.

v2.31.0
  - NEW player movement controllers (Add Component > ...):
    - First Person Controller: mouse-look (yaw turns the body, pitch tilts a
      child Camera) + WASD + jump. Uses a Rigidbody3D if present.
    - Third Person Controller: an orbit camera (mouse + wheel zoom), WASD
      movement relative to the camera, and the body turns to face where it moves.
    - Both drive a sibling Character's animation from movement automatically
      (idle / walk / run / jump), and both save/load with the scene.
  - NEW "First Person" starter template (New Project): a blocky Character you
    control in first person, with a camera at eye height and crates to explore.
  - Polished the New Project template picker: organized into sections (Blank /
    3D / 2D / UI & systems) as a tidy two-column grid of cards with descriptions
    on hover.

v2.30.0
  - Character creator: focused on the Unturned (blocky) body and went deep on
    customization.
    - Removed the Minecraft and RuneScape styles, and the Build / Head Size
      sliders. The body is the blocky Unturned style; Height is the one
      proportion slider.
    - TONS more options:
      - Clothing: shirt (tank / short / long sleeve), legs (trousers / shorts),
        belt, jacket, gloves.
      - Hair: 8 styles (short, long, mohawk, bun, spiky, afro, ponytail, buzz)
        and beards (full / goatee / mustache).
      - Accessories: 8 hats (cap, helmet, top hat, wizard, beanie, cowboy, crown,
        bandana), eyewear (glasses / sunglasses), mask, scarf, shoulder pads,
        backpack, cape.
      - 11 colors: skin, shirt, pants, shoes, hair, eyes, hat, jacket, gloves,
        belt, backpack.
    - Still rigged with the 15-bone skeleton + idle / walk / run / wave / jump.

v2.29.0
  - NEW stylized Character creator (Create > 3D > Character), the kind of
    character maker games ship with:
    - 3 art styles in one component, switchable from a Style dropdown:
      Minecraft (boxy, big cubic head), Unturned (boxy with detail), and
      RuneScape (rounded low-poly with a detailed face + fingered hands).
    - Rigged: a 15-bone humanoid skeleton with rigid skinning, so the character
      can be posed and plays procedural animations -- Idle, Walk, Run, Wave, Jump.
    - Lots of customization: height, build and head-size sliders; skin, shirt,
      pants, shoes, hair, eyes, hat and backpack colors; hair styles
      (short/long/mohawk/bun), shirt styles (tank/short/long sleeve), hat styles
      (cap/helmet/top hat/wizard), plus glasses, backpack, cape and beard toggles.
    - Saves/loads with the scene.

v2.28.0
  - Removed the procedural Character creator (the CharacterBody component, the
    humanoid mesh generators, the bundled MakeHuman base mesh, and its editor UI
    and serialization). It wasn't meeting expectations, so it's been pulled to
    keep the engine focused. Existing scenes that referenced a character simply
    drop that component on load; everything else is unaffected. The general
    movement components (Character Controller 2D / 3D) are untouched.

v2.27.0
  - Character: a big leap in realism, matching MakeHuman's look.
    - NEW skin "matcap" (lit-sphere) shading in the software renderer. The base
      mesh always had a fully-modelled face (eye sockets, nose, lips, ears), but
      flat shading washed it out into a smooth blob. The matcap bakes a soft,
      skin-like lighting response so all that surface relief now reads clearly --
      this is the same technique MakeHuman uses. Generated procedurally, so it
      ships inside the exe (no asset files).
    - NEW eyeballs (white + colored iris + pupil) fitted into the modelled eye
      sockets, plus slim, subtle eyebrows. The face finally looks alive.
    - Default character is now bald (like MakeHuman's default); turn on Hair in
      the inspector for hairstyles. Hair styles were reworked with a proper high
      hairline instead of a bowl cut.
    - Height now makes the character TALLER, not just uniformly bigger: it scales
      mostly vertically (with a gentle width increase), and its range is wider.
  - Renderer: MeshRenderer gained a `matcap` slot; images can be registered
    in-memory (RegisterTexture) and Image gained UV Sample().

v2.26.0
  - Character: the DEFAULT character is now the MakeHuman base figure itself,
    with natural human proportions. Previously the default sliders (lean build,
    long legs, narrow waist/hips) compounded and stretched the base mesh into a
    thin, lanky shape that looked nothing like MakeHuman.
    - All proportion sliders now default to 1.0 (= the unmodified base mesh).
    - Gender and Muscle are symmetric about their midpoint, so the neutral
      setting passes the base mesh through unchanged.
    - The sliders still morph the body from this true-to-MakeHuman starting
      point. Create a fresh Character (Create > 3D > Character) to see it.

v2.25.1
  - Character: fixed the customization sliders tearing/deforming the base mesh.
    Morphs are now smooth, continuous deformations (gender, muscle, shoulders,
    hips, waist, leg length, head, height, build) so the body stays intact.

v2.25.0
  - Character: MakeHuman-style customization of the real human base mesh.
    - NEW Gender slider (Female <-> Male): reshapes shoulders, hips, waist and
      adds a bust toward female — one slider visibly switches body type.
    - NEW Muscle slider (Slim <-> Muscular): limb + torso girth.
    - All the proportion sliders now actually MORPH the base mesh by region —
      Shoulder Width, Hip Width, Waist, Arm/Leg Length & Thickness, Head Size,
      Neck Length, Height, Build — instead of just scaling a blob.
  - Removed the old body styles (Low Poly / Seamless / Parts); the anatomical
    human base mesh is now the one and only body.

v2.24.0
  - Character: REAL human body. The Character creator now uses a proper
    anatomical human base mesh (the MakeHuman CC0 base, embedded in the engine)
    instead of procedural blobs — correct anatomy, hands with fingers, feet, a
    real head. It's tinted by region into clothing (shirt / trousers / shoes /
    skin / hair) and scaled by the Height / Build / Body Depth sliders.
  - This is the default ("Realistic" body style). The old Low Poly / Seamless /
    Parts styles are still available (uncheck Realistic in the Character
    inspector). The base mesh is embedded in the .exe, so it ships via the normal
    auto-update with no extra files.

v2.23.0
  - Model import: File > "Import Model (.obj)..." loads a Wavefront .OBJ with its
    UVs and .mtl diffuse texture (map_Kd) as a textured object. (Also the
    mechanism a built-in base-mesh character generator would use.)

v2.22.0
  - Character: new default "Low Poly" body — a real flat-shaded polygonal mesh
    built from cross-section loops (torso loft + tapered limb tubes + simple
    head), rather than a smooth blob. Toggle in the Character inspector
    ("Body style": Low Poly / Seamless / Parts). Saved with scenes & presets.

v2.21.6
  - Character: big silhouette fix (verified by rendering it directly). The figure
    was far too WIDE (huge shoulders + flaring hips = a triangle/blob). Now: much
    narrower shoulders/chest/hips, leaner arms held in a clear A-pose (so arms
    read as separate from the torso), tapered limbs, and a defined waist. Reads
    as a proper human.
  - NOTE: this changes the DEFAULTS for new characters. An existing character in
    your scene keeps its own saved sliders — click "Reset" in its Character
    inspector (or create a new Character) to get the new proportions.

v2.21.5
  - Character: default build is now athletic ("bulky but lean") instead of soft —
    broader shoulders/chest, a tighter defined waist, narrower hips, slightly
    thicker (muscular) arms and legs, and less front-to-back bulk. Reads as a
    fit, low-body-fat figure rather than a chubby one.

v2.21.4
  - Character: major proportion fix so the body reads as an adult human, not a
    chibi/Roblox figure.
    - Head is now correctly sized — the figure is ~7 heads tall (it was ~5.4,
      which is why it looked toy-like).
    - Shoulders attach at a realistic width instead of sticking out
      superhero-wide; arms now hang from a natural shoulder point.
    - Head reshaped into a human skull: rounded cranium -> cheekbones -> a
      narrower jaw and a small chin (was a plain ball).

v2.21.3
  - Character: limbs are no longer uniform "capsules" — arms and legs now TAPER
    organically (thick shoulder/thigh -> thin wrist/ankle) via a proper tapered-
    capsule shape, so the body reads far more humanoid and less tube-like.

v2.21.2
  - Character: real separated FINGERS — each digit is now a slim two-segment
    capsule (with a knuckle curl) plus an opposed thumb, so the hand reads as a
    proper hand, not a lump.
  - Toward a clean anatomical "base mesh" look: leaner athletic default build,
    longer arms and legs, narrower waist, and an A-pose stance (arms angled out).
    Arm Spread now goes up to 50 deg for a full T/A pose.
  - New "Base Mesh" preset: a lean, uniform-gray, A-posed clay figure (like a 3D
    modelling reference) in one click.

v2.21.1
  - Character proportion refit (less "blobby balloon", more human):
    - Slimmer torso (narrower chest/ribcage/waist/pelvis and less front-to-back
      bulk) so it no longer reads as a wide balloon.
    - Longer arms and thinner forearms; noticeably smaller, better-proportioned
      hands (they were oversized mitts).
    - Slightly larger head and narrower default shoulders so the head reads in
      proportion.
    - Backed the body smoothing down to one gentle pass (two passes had inflated
      it).

v2.21.0
  - NEW: Skeletal rig — pose the character by rotating bones. The body is skinned
    to a 17-bone humanoid skeleton (linear-blend skinning), so bending a bone
    bends the limb and child bones follow (forward kinematics). In the Character
    inspector: "Pose (skeleton rig)" -> pick a bone, drag Rotation; Reset Bone /
    Reset Pose. Poses save with the scene and character presets.
  - Less blocky: the seamless body now gets a Taubin smoothing pass that melts
    the voxel facets off its silhouette (rounder shape, no extra triangles).
  - Stricter "stays human" limits across the board (build, depth, limb thickness,
    waist, head, limb lengths) for more believable proportions.

v2.20.0
  - Character realism pass:
    - Real HANDS: four fingers + a thumb on each hand (built at full detail and
      oriented along the forearm) instead of a featureless stump.
    - Skin & clothing now have a soft specular SHEEN (a lifelike highlight)
      rather than looking like flat matte clay.
  - (Toward a fully rigged/skinned character: that's a larger system; for now
    "Split into Parts" on a character gives you a poseable per-limb rig.)

v2.19.3
  - Character: a more natural, lifelike pose. The arms now rest with a soft
    elbow bend (forearms angled slightly forward and inward) instead of hanging
    as stiff straight rods — the figure reads as a relaxed person rather than a
    mannequin.

v2.19.2
  - Character: more realistic + more constrained.
    - Slimmer neck, a defined upper-abdomen, and a narrower waist for a clearer
      natural torso taper.
    - Fixed bare "orange ankle" gap between trousers and shoes (the ankle now
      matches the trousers).
    - Tighter "stays human" limits across every slider, plus new coupling: hips
      can't read wider than the shoulders and the waist can't exceed the chest,
      so proportions always stay believable.
  - Performance: auto-performance now engages a little sooner, and the seamless
    body uses a slightly lighter resolution — smoother frame rate per character.

v2.19.1
  - Character: more human, less "square/blobby" silhouette.
    - Shoulders now SLOPE from the neck down to rounded deltoids (added a
      trapezius muscle + narrowed the flat clavicle slab) instead of forming a
      square shoulder bar.
    - Feet are now flat, elongated shoe shapes (heel -> tapered toe) rather than
      bulbous blobs; slimmer ankles.
    - Higher seamless-body resolution (52) for a smoother surface.

v2.19.0
  - Performance: big FPS win with several characters in a scene.
    - The seamless character body is now drawn single-sided (it's a closed
      surface), so the renderer backface-culls ~half its triangles.
    - New "Auto performance" (View menu, on by default): when a scene gets heavy
      it automatically drops 2x anti-aliasing to keep the editor smooth.
  - Auto-update: "Automatically install future updates" option (in the Update
    window). When on, the engine checks at startup and — after SAVING your open
    scene — downloads and installs the new build on its own, then relaunches.
    Your AA / performance / auto-update preferences now persist between sessions.
  - Character: more anatomical detail (pectorals + calf muscles) and stricter
    "stays human" limits — every proportion clamps to a tighter human range.

v2.18.0
  - Character Creator: a thorough anatomical pass on the seamless body so it
    reads as a real human:
    - Torso now has proper structure — clavicle/traps, pecs/upper chest, ribcage,
      a narrow waist and a pelvis, blended into one continuous trunk (a natural
      male V-taper instead of two stacked balls).
    - Limbs are anatomical: deltoid shoulder caps, a bicep tapering to a forearm,
      defined elbows; glutes, thighs tapering through the knee to the calf.
    - Real hands (flattened palm + thumb) instead of round stumps, and proper
      forward-pointing feet (sole + toe).
  - "Stays human" restrictions: every proportion is now clamped to a believable
    human range when the character is built, so dragging any slider to an extreme
    can't turn it into a blob. (Arm/Leg Spread sliders tightened to match.)

v2.17.4
  - Updates now check automatically on startup, in the background (no UI freeze).
    If a newer build is published, the Update window pops up on its own — no need
    to open Help > Check for Updates and wait. (Manual check is still there.)
  - Character Creator (seamless body): added shoulder and hip "bridge" masses so
    the arms and legs always fuse into the torso — fixes the see-through holes at
    the armpits (and hips) when the arms/legs are spread out.

v2.17.3
  - Character Creator: FIXED the seamless body collapsing to just a tiny head.
    The smooth-union (smooth-min) blend had its interpolation swapped, so the
    distance field never went negative and the body surface was never generated
    — only the appended head showed. The seamless body now builds the full
    figure as one continuous skin, as intended.

v2.17.2
  - Character Creator: the seamless single-surface body is now the DEFAULT for
    new characters — this is the fix for the "blobby"/snowman look. Instead of
    assembling separate overlapping balls (chest, waist, hips, shoulders), the
    whole body is now fused into ONE continuous skin via smooth-union SDFs +
    Surface Nets, so there are no pinches between parts. Stronger blend and a
    bit more resolution (44) too.
  - NOTE for existing characters: a character saved before this update keeps its
    old setting. Turn on "Seamless Body" in the Character inspector to convert
    it (it's on by default for new ones). The seamless body doesn't animate its
    limbs — uncheck it if you need Walk/Run/Wave/etc.

v2.17.1
  - Terrain: prop scattering (trees, rocks, bushes) — like Unity's tree/detail
    painting. In the Terrain inspector pick a prop (Tree / Pine / Rock / Bush),
    a count, a random scale range, a max slope and a height band, then hit
    Scatter to drop them onto the surface (each placed on the ground with random
    yaw + scale, skipping slopes that are too steep). "Clear Scattered" removes
    them again. Scattered props are real child objects, so they save with the
    scene and can be edited individually.
  - New procedural primitives: Tree, Pine, Rock and Bush — colored and
    smooth-shaded — also available under Create > 3D.

v2.17.0
  - Terrain Creator (Unity-style) — a big upgrade to the Terrain component:
    - Procedural generation from Perlin/fractal noise, with one-click presets:
      Mountains, Hills, Plains, Plateau (mesas), Islands — plus Amplitude,
      Frequency and Detail (octaves) controls and a re-roll Generate button.
    - Sculpt brush modes: Raise/Lower (Shift inverts), Smooth, Flatten (level to
      the height under the cursor) and Set Height (level to a target). Drag in
      the 3D view; Radius + Strength sliders.
    - Auto-color "layers": each face is tinted by elevation and slope — water,
      sand, grass, rock (on steep faces) and snow (on peaks) — with editable
      layer colors and Water/Snow level + Rock slope thresholds.
    - Terrain now uses smooth (Gouraud) shading, so rolling hills look rounded
      instead of faceted.
    - New terrains start as an 80m rolling-hills landscape at 64 resolution.
    All new settings are saved with the scene (older scenes still load).

v2.16.42
  - Renderer: smooth (Gouraud) shading. Meshes that carry per-vertex normals are
    now lit per-pixel across each face instead of one flat color per triangle —
    so curved surfaces look genuinely round, not faceted. This is the real fix
    for "blocky": it needs no extra geometry, so it costs no frames.
  - Character Creator: characters now ship smooth normals (computed across part
    seams, so the joins between body parts disappear too) and the default
    subdivision is back to 1 — smoother-looking AND faster than the previous
    flat-shaded subdivision-2 build (which had tanked the FPS).
  - Per-face material colors stay crisp (skin vs. shirt vs. hair) while the
    lighting is smooth — best of both.

v2.16.41
  - Character Creator: a big step toward a realistic ("GTA-style"), less-blocky
    character:
    - Smoother by default — new characters now build at subdivision 2, and the
      head, arms and legs use higher-resolution base geometry so curves read as
      curves, not facets. (Dial subdivision down in the inspector if you need
      the frames back.)
    - Realistic default proportions — smaller head (~7.5-head build), broader
      shoulders, narrower hips and a tapered athletic waist, longer legs.
    - Natural relaxed hands — fingers are now a softly closed rounded hand with
      a thumb instead of splayed cubes (no more "claw").
    - Cleaner neck/shoulder join via a trapezius blend, and a relaxed default
      arm/leg stance.

v2.16.40
  - Character Creator: tapered torso — broad chest narrowing to a waist (a
    natural V-shape) instead of a straight tube, on both the part-based and
    the seamless body.
  - Character Creator: new "Arm Gap" and "Leg Gap" sliders to set the lateral
    spacing of the limbs from the body (move arms in/out, widen/narrow stance)
    independently of shoulder/hip width and spread angle.

v2.16.39
  - Character Creator: much smaller shoulder and hip joints (they were too big
    and ball-like) — now just fill the seam between limb and body.

v2.16.38
  - Character Creator: smaller shoulder joints (they were too bulky) — they still
    bridge the arm to the torso without ballooning the shoulders.

v2.16.37
  - Character Creator: SEAMLESS BODY mode.
    * New "Seamless Body (smooth skin)" toggle fuses the body masses into ONE
      continuous surface (via signed-distance fields + Surface Nets) instead of
      visibly joined primitives — a real smooth character skin. The detailed head
      is kept on top, and per-region colors (skin/shirt/pants/shoes) carry over.
    * "Smooth Quality" slider controls the surface resolution.
    * Heavier to build, so limb animation is disabled while in this mode (use the
      primitive mode for animated characters; seamless for hero/static).

v2.16.36
  - Character Creator detail pass:
    * Hands now have three fingers + a thumb (not a plain block).
    * Feet get a rounded toe.
    * Head has a jaw/chin so it reads more like a face than a ball.
    * Thicker default arms/legs (less stick-like).

v2.16.35
  - Character Creator: more adult proportions — smaller head and longer legs so
    the default figure is taller and less stubby (use the Height/Head Size/Leg
    Length sliders to taste).

v2.16.34
  - Character Creator: fixed detached hands — the arm now hangs from the
    shoulder and the hand sits at the wrist (no more floating palm squares).
    Arms are a touch longer for a more natural reach.

v2.16.33
  - Character Creator:
    * "Split into Parts" button — bakes the character into one child GameObject
      per body part (Head, Neck, Torso, Hips, Arm.L/R, Leg.L/R), each its own
      selectable/movable/riggable object with its own mesh.
    * Fixed arm/leg spread going the wrong way (now spreads outward).
    * Bigger shoulder & hip joints that bridge the limbs to the body (no more
      detached shoulders).

v2.16.32
  - Character Creator fixes:
    * Fixed the "see-through" body — the character now renders solid (the
      assembled parts are drawn double-sided).
    * Slimmer, more human proportions (thinner limbs, tapered torso).
    * Proper hands (palm + thumb) and feet (ankle + forward sole/toes) instead
      of plain balls and flat blocks.
  - View > "3D Anti-aliasing (2x)" toggle — turn AA off to boost FPS on heavy
    or highly-subdivided scenes.

v2.16.31
  - Character Creator: much smoother, more cohesive bodies.
    * Torso and hips are now rounded (capsule/ellipsoid) instead of boxes.
    * Shoulder and hip joints bridge the limbs to the body (no more gaps /
      "floating blobs").
    * New Taubin smoothing rounds without shrinking, and welds seams across parts
      so subdivision actually smooths the whole figure (no hard creases).
    * Max Subdivisions raised to 4 for extra-smooth high-poly.
  - Renderer: 2x supersampled anti-aliasing for all 3D views (editor Scene/Game
    and the player) — much smoother edges.

v2.16.30
  - Character Creator: "Auto (move)" animation.
